channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: bachChe rAv
item.description:
Naib Dewan Bachhe Rao : 1744 - 1804 (Living)

Naib Dewan Bachhe Rao (also referred to as Butcherow by Mark Wilks; butcharow by Francis Buchanan; Butche Row by Arthur Wellesley; and Buche Rao by others; the spelling now adopted is based on the way the name is written in Haidar Nama and other Kannada publications) was the son of Khande Rao Banaji (Appendix VI).

Although Haidar killed Khande Rao and punished his partisans, he did not harm his family members. Bachhe Rao was born in1744 (?) and was 17 years old when his father, Khande Rao, was killed. He was aware of the misunderstanding between his father and Haidar; the efforts made by Khande Rao to enlist the help of the Marhatta and the English armies to drive Haidar out of Mysore kingdom; the gun fire at Srirangapatna and escape of Haidar; subsequent wars and surrender of his father to Haidar.

item.title: khANDe rAv
item.description:
Khande Rao Banaji - 1730 - 1762 ( Interpolation - See events )

Khande Rao (also referred to as Kunde Row by Mark Wilks; Canero by the French writer M. M. De La Tour; Conderow by Captain Francis Robson, an English writer; Panderau by Eloy Jose Correa Peixoto, a Portugese writer; Khandi Rao by Ramachandra Rao Punganuri in his memoir on Haider Ali translated from Marathi to English by Charles Philip Brown, and Khande Rao Banaji in the Records of the Peshwa Daftar, Pune), was closely associated with the history of Mysore as a loyal officer who made a valiant effort to restore the Mysore kingdom to Krishnaraja Wodeyar II from Haidar Ali, the usurper.+++(5)+++ Khande Rao was a key figure who could have changed the course of Mysore history if he had succeeded in his effort to drive Haidar Ali out of the kingdom.

item.title: दर्भङ्गा-सम-वेद-प्रतिष्ठा
item.description:
Source: TW

सामवेदीय सन्ध्यावन्दन और दरभङ्गामहाराजका प्रसङ्ग।

मैथिलब्राह्मण या तो छान्दोग्यसामवेदी या तो वाजसनेययजुर्वेदी होते हैं। शाण्डिल्यगोत्रीय राजदरभङ्गा परिवार सामवेदी था। किन्तु यजुर्वेदके वर्चस्वके कारण और सामवेदीयोंके अल्पसङ्खयक होनेके कारणवश सामवेदका अध्ययन वहाँ तब दुर्लभ था। सामवेदी या तो काशी जाकर स्वशाखाका अध्ययन करते या क्षेत्रमें प्रचलित परशाखाका। तत्पश्चात् महाराजरामेश्वरसिंहने दक्षिणसे सामवेदीयोंको आमन्त्रितकर एक पाठशाला लगवाई।

पुत्र महाराजकुमार-कामेश्वरसिंह और उनके सहोदरका जब उपनयन हुआ
तब सन्ध्यावन्दन सिखाने हेतु तथा प्रातः-सायं सन्ध्या करवाने हेतु
उसी पाठशालाके छात्र सामवैदिकपण्डित महेन्द्रनाथझागुरुजीको नियुक्त किया था।
वो सामवैदिक गुरुजी सामगानमें जितने शीतल थे उतने ही क्रोधमें दुर्वासावतार।
